Continuous Improvement Manager
Michael Page Procurement & Supply Chain
FeaturedEasy Apply
Continuous Improvement Manager
1 week ago by Michael Page Procurement & Supply Chain
Michael Page Procurement & Supply Chain jobs
£60,000 - £70,000 per annum
Stockport, Cheshire
Permanent, full-time
Continuous Improvement Manager role based in Stockport. Working for w well established, market leading manufacturing company.

Client Details

Our client is a well established, growing manufacturing company who are a market leader in their field.

Description

· Lead change initiatives across all areas of the business by identifying, quantifying, and managing projects from operations, manufacturing, supply chain, and support functions.

· Develop the Continuous Improvement (CI) strategy and deploy lean tools and techniques to meet business requirements, focusing on process improvements in Safety, quality, cost, service, and inventory management whilst growing the people within.

· Implement best practice lean tools and techniques within the business, including Kaizen / Lean Six Sigma, by coaching and training managers, supervisors, and staff; lead Kaizen and 5S events to ensure methodologies align with the business and customer demands.

· Achieve annualized project savings as outlined in the business strategy/growth plans; coordinate project activities during profit planning and support the Operations team with project management and departmental goals.

· Continuously enhance project management techniques and coach others in their use, ensuring clear management and timely closure of projects, with accurate plans and concise, high-quality status presentations.

· Support and reduce business issues through root cause analysis and corrective action plans for all areas of the business.

· Engage the entire organization to promote Lean/CI benefits and foster a culture of continuous improvement as a core business ethos.

· Maintain a flexible approach to CI, adapting to the changing needs of the business to achieve goals and objectives inline with the full business strategy agreed at senior level.

· Demonstrate proficiency in process improvement tools such as Value Stream Mapping (VSM), Standard Work, and 5S; coordinate self-assessments and implement action plans to address gaps.

· Develop and advance tools and methodologies as appropriate to meet business requirements.

· Deliver projects and plans that achieve annualized savings, increase OEE as defined by business objectives

· Lead, train, and coach others in lean tools and techniques, building expertise throughout the business and fostering a culture of continuous improvement to meet all business KPIs.

· Own and manage the Kaizen calendar, ensuring deployment and support of activities throughout the business.

· Support Value Analysis/Value Engineering (VAVE) events and activities to achieve targeted cost reductions where appropriate.

Profile

· You will be a proven process / continuous improvement manager within a manufacturing environment.

· You may have come from a production, operations or quality background within manufacturing originally but will now be an established continuous improvement manager

· Relevant proven experience and or six sigma trained (demonstrated)

· Demonstrated experience within a manufacturing/warehouse and or engineering environment

· Motivated to deliver/achieve with a engaging approach and take people with them on the journey

· Ability to work with all levels of the business and set clear pathways vs strategy

· Demonstrates a collaborative approach

· Experience in working with and training cross-functional groups

· IT literate - proficient in SAP use of SharePoint and able to bring new ideas technology into the business

Job Offer

£60,000 to £70,000 plus bonus and benefits

salary: £60,000 - £70,000 per annum plus bonus and benefits
shortCompanyReason: Well established, market leading manufacturing company
shortRoleReason: Lead continuous improvement initiatives and lean strategy deployment
companySummary: A well established, growing manufacturing company and market leader in their field.
roleSummary: Lead change initiatives, develop and deploy continuous improvement strategies using lean tools, achieve project savings, and foster a culture of continuous improvement across the business.
headlineAttraction: Continuous Improvement Manager role with a market leading manufacturing company in Stockport
rolePurpose: To lead and implement continuous improvement initiatives across the business to enhance safety, quality, cost, service, and inventory management while developing people.
advertSummary: Seeking a Continuous Improvement Manager to lead lean and Six Sigma initiatives, drive process improvements in safety, quality, cost, service, and inventory, and coach teams to meet business KPIs in a manufacturing environment.
personalisedIntro: I bring hands-on experience leading continuous improvement initiatives that enhance operational performance and cost efficiency. This role aligns with my background in deploying lean tools and coaching teams to embed a culture of continuous improvement across manufacturing and supply chain functions.
whyThisRole: Leading change initiatives and deploying lean strategies across multiple business areas is exactly where I add value. I’m motivated by roles that require both hands-on project delivery and developing people through coaching and training, which this position clearly demands.
coreResponsibilities: Lead change initiatives across operations, manufacturing, supply chain, and support functions
Develop and deploy CI strategy using lean tools and techniques
Coach and train managers and staff in lean methodologies
Support operations with project management and departmental goals
Conduct root cause analysis and corrective actions
Promote Lean/CI culture throughout the organization
Maintain flexibility to adapt CI approach to business needs
Use process improvement tools like VSM and Standard Work
Support VAVE events for cost reduction
essentialRequirements: Proven continuous improvement management experience in manufacturing
Experience in production, operations or quality background
Experience in manufacturing, warehouse or engineering environment
Ability to engage and motivate teams
Experience working with all business levels
IT literate with SAP and SharePoint proficiency

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Driving lean transformation and operational excellence in manufacturing environments
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I bring hands-on experience leading continuous improvement initiatives that enhance operational performance and cost efficiency. This role aligns with my background in deploying lean tools and coaching teams to embed a culture of continuous improvement across manufacturing and supply chain functions.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: The opportunity to join a well-established, market-leading manufacturer that is growing excites me because it offers a platform to apply structured improvement strategies at scale. The company’s commitment to operational excellence and continuous improvement fits well with my experience and approach.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: Leading change initiatives and deploying lean strategies across multiple business areas is exactly where I add value. I’m motivated by roles that require both hands-on project delivery and developing people through coaching and training, which this position clearly demands.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Lead change initiatives and improve operational resilience during growth
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: This example shows my ability to strengthen operational discipline and delivery reliability in a manufacturing environment during a growth phase, directly relevant to the role’s focus on lean deployment and operational improvement.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Hero metric and supporting point on manufacturing leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I led operations through a manufacturing scale-up, improving delivery discipline and operational resilience.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Achieve project savings and improve financial control
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: This demonstrates my ability to drive financial improvements through tighter operational control, aligning with the role’s emphasis on cost reduction and project savings.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Supporting point on cost and financial impact
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I supported tighter operational control that contributed to a £149.9k profit improvement and better cash flow.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Ride compliance and operator progression tracking
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Coach and train teams, promote compliance and continuous improvement culture
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: This example highlights my experience creating practical systems to support compliance and team progression, reflecting the role’s need for coaching and promoting continuous improvement ethos.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Supporting point on training and culture fit
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I developed a compliance tracking system that improved operational oversight and team progression.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: This role requires someone who can lead and embed continuous improvement across manufacturing and support functions, balancing project delivery with people development. It’s about driving measurable savings, improving quality and efficiency, and fostering a lean culture that adapts to evolving business needs.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Lead change initiatives across operations and manufacturing
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I managed day-to-day operations during a manufacturing scale-up, improving delivery discipline and operational resilience.
experienceMappings.relevance: Shows direct experience leading operational improvements in manufacturing growth environments.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Achieve project savings and cost reduction
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I supported tighter financial and operational controls that resulted in significant profit improvement and working capital release.
experienceMappings.relevance: Demonstrates ability to deliver financial benefits through operational improvements.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Coach and train managers and staff in lean methodologies
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I created a user input system to monitor compliance and progression, supporting team development and operational standards.
experienceMappings.relevance: Reflects experience in coaching and enabling teams to adopt continuous improvement practices.

focusAreasToBring.title: Structured Lean Deployment
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would apply proven lean tools like Kaizen, 5S, and Value Stream Mapping to identify waste and improve process flow across manufacturing and supply chain.

focusAreasToBring.title: Financial and Operational Discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I focus on embedding tighter operational controls that directly contribute to cost savings and improved working capital.

focusAreasToBring.title: People Development and Engagement
focusAreasToBring.summary: I prioritize coaching and training to build capability and foster a continuous improvement mindset throughout all levels of the business.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background in manufacturing operations and continuous improvement aligns well with the role’s requirements. I’ve led operational improvements during growth phases, delivered financial benefits through tighter controls, and coached teams to adopt lean practices, matching the role’s blend of project delivery and people development.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: In the first year, I expect to focus on deploying lean tools to improve process efficiency and cost control, leading Kaizen events to engage teams, and embedding a culture of continuous improvement. I would also support project management discipline to ensure timely delivery of savings and operational targets.
companyHighlights: Well established and growing manufacturing market leader
Committed to operational excellence and continuous improvement
Collaborative and engaging culture focused on lean transformation
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work collaboratively and engage people at all levels to take them on the improvement journey. My approach is practical and flexible, adapting lean methods to fit the business context while fostering a culture where continuous improvement is part of everyday work.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would immerse myself in current operations, review existing continuous improvement initiatives, and build relationships with key stakeholders across functions.
I would identify priority improvement projects, lead initial Kaizen events, and begin coaching managers and teams on lean tools and techniques.
I would establish a structured Kaizen calendar, support project management rigor, and work to embed continuous improvement behaviors across the business.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’m ready to lead practical, measurable continuous improvement that strengthens operations and builds capability across your manufacturing business.
closingProofPoints: Led operational improvements during manufacturing scale-up, enhancing delivery and resilience.
Supported financial improvements delivering nearly £150k profit uplift and better cash flow.
Developed compliance tracking systems that improved team progression and operational oversight.
contentNotes: Advert provides strong detail on role responsibilities and company context, enabling focused content.
Evidence bank includes relevant manufacturing and operational improvement examples, though not all directly manufacturing; selection prioritizes closest matches.
Limited direct Six Sigma project examples; emphasis placed on lean and operational leadership experience.
Company culture signals are limited but suggest collaboration and continuous improvement ethos; content reflects this.
